title: Best VPN for Mac in 2026: Tested on macOS Sequoia
description: The best VPNs for Mac in 2026, tested on macOS Sequoia. NordVPN, ExpressVPN, Surfshark, Mullvad and ProtonVPN compared for speed, privacy, and native macOS app quality.
content:
## What Makes a VPN Good for Mac?

Mac VPNs have some different requirements compared to Windows or iOS. macOS has tighter security controls, including Gatekeeper and System Integrity Protection, which means VPN apps have to play by stricter rules. A VPN app that integrates with macOS's native Network Extension framework behaves better than one that relies on tunneling workarounds.

The three things that matter most for a Mac VPN:

**Native Apple Silicon support.** An app built for Intel that runs under Rosetta 2 uses more CPU and more battery on M1/M2/M3 chips. The best VPNs now publish universal binaries that run natively on both Intel Macs and Apple Silicon.

**macOS menu bar integration.** The best Mac VPN apps live in the menu bar, not as a separate full-screen app. One click to connect, one click to switch server. Apps that require opening a separate window every time are friction.

**Split tunneling on macOS.** Split tunneling lets you route only specific apps through the VPN while the rest of your traffic goes direct. On Mac this is particularly useful: route your browser through the VPN for privacy while keeping your video calls going direct to avoid latency.

## The 5 Best VPNs for Mac in 2026

### 1. NordVPN — Best Overall for Mac

NordVPN's macOS app is the most complete package. It supports Apple Silicon natively, sits in the menu bar, and includes Threat Protection Lite, which blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level without routing traffic through the VPN (it works even when the VPN is disconnected). This is a feature no other VPN in this list offers.

The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard) delivers the best speeds in this category. In tests from Western Europe, speeds dropped less than 8% on nearby servers compared to a direct connection. Split tunneling is fully supported and lets you choose apps to exclude from the VPN tunnel.

**macOS app quality:** native Apple Silicon binary, menu bar first design, split tunneling by app
**Price:** $3.99/month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 10

### 2. ExpressVPN — Best for Speed on Mac

ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol was designed with mobile and Mac in mind. It handles network transitions (sleep/wake, network switching) faster than any other protocol. When you open your MacBook lid, ExpressVPN reconnects in under 2 seconds on average.

The macOS app is clean and includes a network lock (kill switch) that actually works on macOS. It also supports split tunneling. The downside: at $6.67/month it's the most expensive option in this roundup, and it caps at 8 simultaneous devices.

**Price:** $6.67/month (yearly plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 8

### 3. Surfshark — Best Budget Mac VPN

Surfshark covers unlimited simultaneous devices, making it the best choice if you have a MacBook, iPhone, iPad, and a Windows PC on the same account. The macOS app supports WireGuard and has CleanWeb for ad and tracker blocking. The interface is straightforward but not as polished as NordVPN or ExpressVPN.

For the price ($2.99/month on a 2-year plan), Surfshark is the hardest to beat on value.

**Price:** $2.99/month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** Unlimited

### 4. ProtonVPN — Best for Privacy-Focused Mac Users

ProtonVPN is developed by the same team behind ProtonMail, which means the company's reputation depends on being genuinely private. All ProtonVPN apps are open source and have been independently audited. For Mac users who are most concerned about the VPN provider itself being trustworthy, ProtonVPN is the answer.

The macOS app supports WireGuard and IKEv2. NetShield blocks ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level. The free tier covers three server locations with unlimited bandwidth. The paid Plus plan ($4.99/month) adds streaming support and 6,000+ servers.

**Price:** Free (3 servers). Plus: $4.99/month
**Simultaneous devices:** 10 (Plus)

### 5. Mullvad — Best for Anonymous Mac VPN Use

Mullvad requires no email address to sign up. You generate an account number and pay. That's it. The macOS app is minimal, functional, and respects macOS security conventions without asking for unusual permissions.

For users who want a Mac VPN that doesn't know who they are, Mullvad is the only credible choice. The tradeoff: there's no streaming unblocking, no dedicated IP option, and fixed pricing at $5/month with no annual discount.

**Price:** $5/month (fixed)
**Simultaneous devices:** 5

## Free VPNs for Mac: What You Should Know

Free VPNs split into two categories: legitimate freemium models (ProtonVPN, Windscribe) and ad/data-monetized ones. Mac users are a high-value demographic and many sketchy free VPN apps are designed specifically to harvest the browsing data of Mac users, who skew toward higher income and business use.

If you want a free Mac VPN, use ProtonVPN's free tier (unlimited bandwidth, three locations) or Windscribe (10 GB/month, 14 locations). Avoid anything that shows ads, asks for excessive permissions on install, or has no clear business model beyond 'free.'

## How to Install a VPN on Mac

Installing any of the above takes under 3 minutes:

1. Download the app from the VPN's website or the Mac App Store
2. Drag to Applications and open
3. Log in or create an account
4. macOS will ask you to allow VPN configurations in System Settings — click Allow
5. Click Connect

For a menu bar shortcut, look for a menu bar icon option in the VPN app settings. All five VPNs in this list support running from the menu bar.

## Mac VPN Speed Test Results

All tests run on a MacBook Pro M3, 1 Gbps connection, testing nearest server in Western Europe:

| VPN | Download (% of base) | Protocol |
|-----|---------------------|----------|
| No VPN | 100% (baseline) | — |
| NordVPN | 92% | NordLynx |
| ExpressVPN | 90% | Lightway |
| Surfshark | 88% | WireGuard |
| ProtonVPN | 85% | WireGuard |
| Mullvad | 87% | WireGuard |

All five are fast enough that the difference is not noticeable in everyday use. The speed gaps only matter for very bandwidth-intensive tasks like downloading large files or 4K streaming, where 85% vs 92% translates to a visible difference.

## The Verdict

**Best overall Mac VPN:** NordVPN. Native Apple Silicon app, Threat Protection that works without VPN active, best speeds in the test.

**Best budget:** Surfshark. Unlimited devices, solid performance, $2.99/month.

**Best free tier:** ProtonVPN. Unlimited bandwidth, no data selling, trustworthy company.

**Best for privacy:** Mullvad. No account, no email, no logs.

**Best for frequent travelers:** ExpressVPN. Lightway protocol reconnects fastest when switching networks.
